data.title: Cryptographic Key Hierarchy
data.description: PRISM enforces robust isolation using HMAC-based Extract-and-Expand Key Derivation (HKDF). A single TPM-bound device root key deterministically expands into distinct functional domains.

data.title: Encryption & Data Isolation
data.description: Each user's biometric vault CID is AES-encrypted. Decentralized access control is inherently mathematical rather than programmatic.

data.title: Distributed Storage Logic
data.description: The system's reliance on IPFS for decentralized storage necessitates a strict zero-knowledge model to prevent unauthorized indexing or retrieval.

phases.title: Private Swarm Isolation
phases.desc: Nodes lacking the cryptographic swarm_key are rejected at the transport layer before participating in the distributed hash table. External nodes cannot index or retrieve vault objects.

phases.title: Zero-Data Paradigm
phases.desc: Only the CID is stored on-chain. Decryption capabilities remain mathematically bound to live biometric reconstruction. Opacity is preserved.

phases.title: Data Anchoring Integrity
phases.desc: Transactions broadcast only after successful pin confirmation across the swarm. Content-addressed storage makes any payload tampering inherently visible by altering the CID.
